description: A research project focusing on distributed formation control, uniform deployment, and assembly-separation behavior for UAVs under local coordinate constraints.

system_model.dynamics: Single-integrator or velocity-controlled model. Control input is velocity in the agent's body frame.
system_model.coordinates: Agents must operate only using local coordinate frames.

observations.bearing_left: bearing to left neighbor in local frame
observations.bearing_right: bearing to right neighbor in local frame

angle_based_control_Li2022.paper: Satellite Formation Flying Control by Using Only Angle Measurements (Li et al. 2022)
reasons: Relies on orbital dynamics (Hill equations), not UAV dynamics
Angle constraints do not uniquely determine 4-agent square
Requires angle rigidity not available in UAV setting

concept: Constructs a virtual angle-bisector direction from left/right distances
Combines uniform deployment term + formation maintenance term
problems: Non-symmetric graph makes theoretical proof difficult

identified_core_problems.uniqueness_issues: Angle constraints alone cannot define quadrilateral formations
identified_core_problems.lack_of_theoretical_framework: Need a provable Lyapunov structure for Test4 or an improved method

A_uniform_deployment_stable_control.goal: Design a provably stable controller using only local distances and bearings

tasks: Linearize at equilibrium and analyze stability

C_LQR_inner_loop.goal: Add stabilizing optimal control for error suppression
C_LQR_inner_loop.notes: Useful for real robots and good for presentations

D_extension_to_defense_scenarios.goal: Integrate formation control with active/passive defense
